questions.q: What is the primary objective of accounting?
questions.answer: The primary objective of accounting is to provide financial information about a business entity.

questions.q: Distinguish between Accounting Equation and Accounting Identity.
questions.answer: Accounting Equation: Assets = Capital + Liabilities. Accounting Identity: Assets = Capital + Liabilities + Drawing (or Owner's drawings).

questions.q: State any two features of Accounting Standards.
questions.answer: Two features of Accounting Standards are: (i) Accounting Standards are formulated by Accounting Standards Board (ASB). (ii) Accounting Standards are formulated to ensure uniformity and comparability of financial statements.

questions.q: What is the difference between Capital and Capital Reserve?
questions.answer: __Capital Reserve is a portion of profit which is set apart for future use, while Capital is the amount invested by the proprietor.__
Materiality refers to the significance or importance of an item in the preparation of financial statements.

questions.q: What is the term 'Double Entry System'?
questions.answer: __Double Entry System is a system of recording financial transactions in two or more accounts.__

questions.q: Explain the main difference between Financial Accounting and Management Accounting.
questions.answer: Financial Accounting provides financial information about a business entity to external users, whereas Management Accounting provides internal users with information to make decisions.
Accounting Period is the period for which financial statements are prepared.
Going Concern refers to the assumption that a business will continue to operate for an indefinite period of time.

questions.q: State the two main principles of Accounting.
questions.answer: Two main principles of Accounting are: (i) Accounting is a language of business. (ii) Accounting is a systematic process.
